original: The first crew arrives at the International Space Station
"Thriller" single released worldwide byMichael Jackson
Baby Shark by Pinkfong becomes the most-watched video on YouTube with over 7.04 billion views
Steve Ditko, American comic artist and co-creator of Spider-Man and Doctor Strange, born in Johnstown, Pennsylvania (d. 2018)
The "Great Emu War" begins: Australian soldiers armed with Lewis Guns seek to cull the Emu population over crop destruction in Campion district, Western Australia
German patent granted to inventor Carl Benz for "vehicle with gas engine operation", the first automobile (No. DE37435C)
Baseball World Series: Chicago Cubs beat Cleveland Indians, 8-7 in Game 7 at Progressive Field, Cleveland; first Cubs' title in 108 years; MVP: Chicago utility Ben Zobrist
Howard Hughesflies "Spruce Goose", a huge wooden airplane for the first and last time
Craig Breedlovedriving FIA-legal four-wheeler, Sonic I, breaks land speed record with a two-run average of 555.483 mph (893.963 km/h) at the Bonneville Salt Flats, Utah
Bill Mosienko, Canadian Hockey Hall of Fame right wing (fastest hat trick in NHL history, 21 seconds 1952; 5-time All Star), born in Winnipeg, Manitoba (d. 1994)

Wayne Gretzkyis sold to the Edmonton Oilers after just 8 games with the Indianapolis Racers of the WHA (scoring 6 points); scores 104 points in 72 games (both teams combined) and is named WHA rookie of the year
US PresidentRonald Reagansigns bill establishing DrMartin Luther King Jr.holiday

Apple Records release the last Beatles song "Now and Then" as a double A-sided single, backed with their first "From Me To You"; the new song was built from an unfinished 1978John Lennondemo and 1995 attempt to complete it that had been abandoned due to technical issues, resolved by new technology
Golden State Warriors register a 162-158 win over the Denver Nuggets at McNichols Arena, with the 320 total points setting an NBA record for most points scored by 2 teams in a non-overtime game
Nevada makes biggest comeback in NCAA football history, overcoming a 35 point 3rd quarter deficit to rally and beat Weber State, 55-49
GeneralGeorge Washington, later 1st US President, bids farewell to his army after the American Revolutionary War
The Levelland UFO Case in Levelland, Texas, generates national publicity, remains one of the most impressive UFO cases in American history
Prodigious Portuguese strikerCristiano Ronaldoscores his 100th goal for Real Madrid in his 105th game for the club in a 2-0 Champions League win over Olympique Lyon
Philadelphia Phillies pitcherSteve Carltonis unanimously selected National League Cy Young Award winner; wins 27 games for Phillies, who as a team win only 59 games
Cheerleading begins in the United States as Johnny Campbell leads the crowd cheering on the football team at the University of Minnesota

NFL  San Diego Chargers Eric Metcalf returns punts for touchdowns of 85 & 67 yards in 38-31 loss at Cincinnati; joins Hall of Famer Jack Christiansen as only players in NFL history to return punts for TDs twice in a game
LA Kings score 5 goals in a span of 5:37, the fastest in NHL team history; rout Hartford Whalers, 8-1 at Hartford Civic Center
(Roberta) "Bobbi" Gibb, American runner (unsanctioned era female winner of Boston Marathon, 1966-68), lawyer, writer, and sculptor, born in Cambridge, Massachusetts
Amar Bose, American electrical engineer, academic (M.I.T.), sound engineer, inventor, and entrepreneur (Bose Corporation), born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania (d. 2013)
Jeffrey Alan Hoffman, American NASA astronaut (STS 51-D, 35, 46, 61, 75) and MIT professor, born in Brooklyn, New York
Rodgers & Hammerstein's television musical "Cinderella", starringBrandy Norwood,Whitney Houston, andBernadette Peterspremieres on ABC-TV
9th Rugby World Cup, Yokohama: South African fly-half Handré Pollard lands 6 penalties and 2 conversions as Springboks beat favourites England, 32-12 for their 3rd title
British McLaren driverLewis Hamiltonfinishes 4th in season-ending Brazilian Grand Prix at Autódromo José Carlos Pace to clinch his first F1 World Drivers Championship by 1 point from Felipe Massa
New York City Marathon: Wilson Kipsang of Kenya wins in 2:10:59; compatriot Mary Keitany wins first of 3 straight women's titles in 2:25:07
New York City Marathon: Margaret Okayo of Kenya wins in 2:22:31 to break her own record; her countryman Martin Lel wins men's race in 2:10:30
Carter Beauford, American drummer (Dave Matthews Band), born in Charlottesville, Virginia
Willie McGee, American baseball outfielder (MLB All Star 1983, 85, 87-88; NL MVP 1985; St Louis Cardinals), born in San Francisco, California
k.d. lang [Kathryn Dawn Lang], Canadian Juno and Grammy Award-winning country-pop singer-songwriter ("Constant Craving"; "Hallelujah"), and human rights activist, born in Edmonton, Alberta
Lynn Nottage, American playwright (first woman playwright to win two Pulitzer Prizes - "Ruined" 2009, "Sweat" 2017), born in Brooklyn, New York
MLB All-Star team led byConnie Mackand includingBabe Ruth,Jimmie FoxxandLou Gehrigstarts 18-game tour against Japanese Big-Six University League

German tennis starBoris Beckerwins his third tournament in 3 weeks in different continents; beats Sergio Casal of Spain 6-4, 6-3, 7-6 in the final of the Paris Open after victories in Japan and Australia
Retired boxerJersey Joe WalcottKO's William Strang in election for Sheriff of Camden County, New Jersey; first African-American to be elected to the position
Apple Records releases "Ringo",Ringo Starr's third studio album, in the US; his biggest commercial and critical success, featuring the singles "Photograph", "You're Sixteen", and "Oh My My"
"BlackadderGoes Forth" final episode "Goodbyeee" airs on BBC-TV starringRowan Atkinsonand Tony Robinson, written by Richard Curtis and Ben Elton
Bletchley Declaration to assess and manage risks of ‘frontier’ AI signed at AI Safety Summit hosted byRishi Sunakat Bletchley Park, UK
Microbiologist Carl R. Woese and scientists from the University of Illinois announce the identification of methanogens, a form of microbial life (Archaea) dating back some 3.5 billion years
Storm Ciarán batters Northern Europe and Southern England with hurricane force winds, leaving 1.2 million in France without power and at least four dead
New York City Marathon: cancelled because of the effects of Hurricane Sandy in NYC the week before
2nd Rugby World Cup Final,Twickenham: Australia beats England, 12-6 with Wallabies fly-half Michael Lynagh landing 2 penalties and a conversion
First test flight of Airbus A330
First regular season game played outside North America by any major professional sports league the NBA's Phoenix Suns beat the Utah Jazz, 119-96 at the Tokyo Metropolitan Gymnasium in Japan
Mexican radio station erroneously reportsMike Tysonhad died in car crash
Harlow Shapley, American astronomer (Sun's position in galaxy), born in Nashville, Missouri (d. 1972)
Melvin Schwartz, American Physicist and Nobel Laureate (neutrinos - subatomic particles with no electric charge and virtually no mass), born in New York City (d. 2006)
Bengt Edlén, Swedish Astrophysicist who specialized in spectroscopy and discovered the source of unidentified emission lines in solar spectrum, born in Gusum, Sweden (d. 2003)
Construction begins on Kingdome, Seattle
Crew of Soyuz 29 returns to Earth aboard Soyuz 31
At 3pm the BBC begins the world's first regular high-definition TV broadcast service from specially constructed studios at Alexandra Palace, North London
KHSD TV channel 11 in Lead, SD (ABC) begins broadcasting
BBC radio comedy "Hancock's Half Hour" debuts starringTony Hancockand Sid James, written by Ray Galton and Alan Simpson
Austro-Hungarian Jewish activistTheodor Herzlarrives in Jerusalem
Coronation of Ras Tafari Makonnen asHaile SelassieI, the 225th Emperor of the Ethiopian Solomonic Dynasty
Dmitri Shostakovich's 1st Symphony premieres in US with Leopold Stokowski leading the Philadelphia Orchestra at The Academy of Music, Philadelpha
Charles Diggs Jr is elected Michigan's 1st black congressman to the House of Representatives
North Dakota becomes 39th & South Dakota becomes 40th state in the United States
